PICO User Manual

Version 1.0

William E. Hart, Cynthia A. Phillips
Discrete Mathematics and Algorithms Dept
Sandia National Laboratories
P.O. Box 5800
Albuquerque, NM
wehart@sandia.gov,caphill@sandia.gov 		Jonathan Eckstein
MSIS Department
Faculty of Management and RUTCOR
Rutgers University
640 Bartholomew Road
Piscataway, NJ 08854
jeckstei@rutcor.rutgers.edu

This document provides a user guide for the PICO software library. PICO (Parallel Integer and Combinatorial Optimizer) is an object-oriented framework for parallel implementation of branch-and-bound algorithms. Using object-oriented techniques, PICO's parallel search engine is cleanly separated from the details of the application and computing platform. This approach allows the same underlying parallel search code to be used on a wide variety of branch-and-bound applications. The PICO software library can be used in several ways: (1) as a linear or integer programming solver launched by AMPL, (2) as a stand-alone linear or integer programming solver, (3) as a framework for developing customized integer programming solvers and (4) as a general-purpose framework for serial and parallel branching algorithms. This user guide illustrates each of these uses of PICO.

Table of Contents

  • Introduction
  • Software Overview
  • Software Installation
  • Extending the PICO Software Framework
  • Application Example
  • Running PICO Executables
  • Using the PICO LP and MILP Solver
  • Customizing the PICO MIP Solver
  • References
  • Acknowledgements
